name: Cranberry Upside-Down Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Grease muffin tins.
3. Combine oats and soy milk in a large bowl. Set aside.
4. Sift together flour, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, cream of tartar, and baking soda. Stir in brown sugar.
5. Whisk egg replacer and water until light and foamy.
6. Combine egg replacer, applesauce, and oat mixture. Mix well. Add dry ingredients and mix just until blended. Do not overmix.
7. Place 2 teaspoons of cranberry sauce in the bottom of each muffin cup. Fill muffin cups 2/3 full with batter.
8. Bake until a toothpick inserted into the middle of a muffin comes out clean, about 15 minutes. Remove from oven and cool for 2 minutes. Invert muffins onto wire racks to finish cooling.
9. Serve warm or at room temperature. Makes 24 muffins
Per muffin: 112 calories, 2 grams protein, 130 milligrams sodium, 24 grams carbohydrates, 0.7 grams fat, 0 cholesterol, 20 milligrams calcium
If using whole eggs instead of egg replacer, do not add the 1/2 cup water.
Source: Vegetarian Gourmet, Autumn 1993
